ip_outcome_note: Card name, National Pokedex no. 385, and all visual descriptors (star-shaped head, ribbon streamers, yellow-white palette, floating posture) match the canonical Jirachi entry. Standard named-Pokemon card; figure identity is unambiguous.

trivia_bullets.text: Team Up (set code SM9) released February 1, 2019, as part of the Sun & Moon series. The set's name references team-based play and introduced the Tag Team GX mechanic, which pairs two Pokemon on a single card. Jirachi appears here as card 99 of 181 printed total, as a supporting non-GX Holo Rare rather than a headline Tag Team. [PokemonTCG.io API: sm9-99]

Jirachi is National Pokedex no. 385, a Steel/Psychic-type Mythical Pokemon introduced in Generation III (Ruby & Sapphire, 2002). Its design is based on the Japanese Tanabata festival — the tuquoise strips hanging from its three head points are tanzaku, the paper strips on which wishes are written and hung from bamboo during Tanabata. [WebSearch: On the Origin of Species: Jirachi, Bulbanews]
Flavor text on this printing: "It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years." [PokemonTCG.io API: sm9-99]

Stellar Wish — the card's Ability — made this Jirachi one of the most widely played non-GX supports during the 2019-2021 Standard format, functioning as a consistent Trainer-search engine at the cost of putting Jirachi to Sleep. Its synergy partner was Escape Board (a Tool card that reduced retreat cost to zero even while Asleep), allowing players to search for a Trainer and then freely retreat. When Escape Board rotated out of Standard, Stellar Wish Jirachi's meta viability declined sharply; it was ranked no. 4 on Pojo's list of top cards lost to the 2022 rotation. The card remains legal in Expanded. [Pojo.com: Top 15 Cards Lost to the 2022 Rotation] [PokemonTCG.io API: sm9-99 legalities]
sources: Pojo.com: Top 15 Pokemon Cards Lost to the 2022 Rotation

trivia_bullets.text: Art by HYOGONOSUKE. TCGPlayer market price (holo): $6.32 as of May 2026, reflecting continued Expanded demand and collector interest in the holo printing. [PokemonTCG.io API: sm9-99 TCGPlayer pricing]

related_cards.name: Escape Board
related_cards.note: The Tool card that made Stellar Wish Jirachi's sleep-then-retreat loop seamless; its rotation effectively retired Jirachi from Standard

related_cards.name: Jirachi-GX
related_cards.note: GX version of Jirachi from the same era; different role in competitive play

related_cards.name: Jirachi Prism Star
related_cards.note: Appears in the same set (TEU) — alternate Jirachi with Prism Star mechanics

related_cards.name: Trainer cards (general)
related_cards.note: Stellar Wish's entire function is to tutor Trainers — any Trainer-dense deck is a Jirachi pairing candidate
